title: Razorpay Org Tree Max Depth
description: Razorpay's HR portal stores the org as a binary tree (each manager has up to two direct reports). The 'depth' of the org tree is the number of nodes along the longest manager-to-IC path. Given the level-order encoded `tree` (with `null` for missing nodes), return the maximum depth.
hints: Recursion: depth(node) = 1 + max(depth(left), depth(right)).
Empty tree ka depth 0 hai — base case sambhal le.
Iterative BFS bhi chalega — har level pe counter bump kar.

title: Flipkart Category Inorder Traversal
description: Flipkart stores its category hierarchy as a binary tree. The data team needs the inorder traversal (left, root, right) of category IDs. Given the level-order encoded `tree`, return the list of values in inorder.
hints: Recursion: inorder(left) -> visit -> inorder(right).
Iterative: explicit stack — push lefts till null, pop, go right.
Empty tree -> empty array.

title: Sharechat Content Preorder Traversal
description: Sharechat's content hierarchy is a binary tree (post -> reply -> nested reply). The moderation queue wants preorder (root, left, right) so parents render before children. Given level-order `tree`, return preorder values.
hints: Visit root, then preorder(left), then preorder(right).
Iterative: stack, push right first then left (LIFO).
Single node -> [val].

title: Zomato Menu Postorder Traversal
description: Zomato's menu is a binary tree. Pricing rollup needs postorder (left, right, root) so children prices are computed before the parent. Given level-order `tree`, return postorder list of values.
hints: Recursion: postorder(left) -> postorder(right) -> visit.
Iterative: two stacks or reverse-preorder trick.
Useful when child results feed into parent.

title: Paytm Merchant Tree Node Count
description: Paytm models its merchant onboarding pipeline as a binary tree. Count the total nodes (merchants in the funnel). Given level-order `tree`, return the count.
hints: DFS: count = 1 + count(left) + count(right).
Empty -> 0.
BFS with queue size accumulation also works.

title: Swiggy Order Tree Total
description: Swiggy stores order amounts in a binary tree (combo splits into items). Sum all node values to compute the cart total. Given level-order `tree`, return the sum of all node values.
hints: DFS aggregator.
sum = node.val + sum(left) + sum(right).
Empty subtree contributes 0.

title: Flipkart Mirror Category Tree
description: Flipkart wants to A/B test a 'right-to-left' UI layout: mirror the entire category binary tree (swap left and right at every node). Given level-order `tree`, return the mirrored tree as a level-order array.
hints: Recursion: invert(left) and invert(right), then swap.
BFS works too — for each dequeued node swap children, enqueue both.
treeToArray(root) for level-order output.

title: Razorpay Symmetric Payment Tree
description: Razorpay flags settlement trees that should be left-right symmetric (mirror image of itself). Given level-order `tree`, return `true` if the tree is symmetric around its centre.
hints: Compare left subtree against mirrored right subtree.
Helper: isMirror(a, b) — both null OK, one null fail, val match + cross-recurse.
Empty tree is symmetric.

title: PhonePe Same Payment Tree
description: PhonePe diff-checks two settlement trees daily. Given two level-order encoded trees `a` and `b`, return `true` if they have the same structure and identical values.
hints: Recurse both in lockstep — both null pass, one null fail.
Values must match before recursing left and right.
treeToArray is not safe — structure matters!

title: Zomato Floor-by-Floor Restaurant Levels
description: Zomato's mall view groups restaurants by floor. Given a binary tree's level-order encoding, return its values level-by-level (each level as its own list).
hints: BFS with a queue — process current level fully before the next.
Capture queue length at the start of each level iteration.
Empty tree -> empty array.

title: Razorpay BST Merchant Lookup
description: Razorpay indexes merchant IDs in a BST for O(log n) lookup. Given the BST `tree` (level-order array, BST property holds) and a target `val`, return the level-order array of the subtree rooted at the matched node, or `[]` if not found.
hints: BST property: target < node -> go left, > -> go right.
Found -> return that subtree.
Use treeToArray on the matched node.

title: Flipkart BST Minimum Price
description: Flipkart stores discounted product prices in a BST. The 'lowest price' shortcut needs the min value. Given BST `tree`, return the smallest value (assume non-empty).
hints: Walk left until null — last node is the min in a BST.
Iterative is cleanest.
Single node -> that value.

title: Swiggy BST Maximum Bill
description: Swiggy keeps order totals in a BST for fast quantile queries. Return the maximum bill amount. Given BST `tree` (non-empty).
hints: Walk right till null — that's the max.
Iterative loop suffices.
Empty case ruled out by constraints.

title: Razorpay Validate BST Tree
description: Razorpay's audit needs to verify a tree adheres to BST property strictly: every node's left subtree values < node < right subtree values. Given level-order `tree`, return `true` if valid BST.
hints: Pass min/max bounds down — comparing only with parent is wrong.
Helper(node, lo, hi). Left -> hi=node.val. Right -> lo=node.val.
Inorder traversal must be strictly increasing — alternative.

title: Flipkart BST Lowest Common Ancestor
description: Flipkart's category BST: find the LCA (deepest node that is ancestor of both `p` and `q`) of two category IDs. BST property holds. Both values are guaranteed to exist. Return the LCA's value.
hints: BST trick: walk down. If both p,q < node -> go left. Both > -> right. Else node is LCA.
Iterative O(h) without recursion.
If p == node or q == node and other is in subtree — node itself is LCA.

title: Razorpay Binary Tree Lowest Common Ancestor
description: In Razorpay's general org tree (not BST), find the LCA of two employee IDs `p` and `q`. Both values exist and are unique. Return the LCA's value.
hints: Recursive: if root is null or matches p/q, return root.
Recurse left and right. If both come back non-null, root is LCA.
Otherwise return whichever side returned non-null.

title: PhonePe Balanced Tree Check
description: PhonePe's index needs the AVL-style balance check: every node's left and right subtree heights differ by at most 1. Given level-order `tree`, return `true` if balanced.
hints: DFS returns height OR -1 (unbalanced sentinel).
If either child returns -1 or |hL - hR| > 1, propagate -1.
Avoids the n^2 trap of computing height at every node.

title: Zomato Menu Tree Diameter
description: Zomato's menu graph diameter = the length (in edges) of the longest path between any two nodes in the binary tree. Return the diameter.
hints: DFS returns height; track best left+right at every node.
Diameter (in edges) = max over all nodes of (heightLeft + heightRight).
Path may not pass through root.

title: Swiggy Root-to-Leaf Target Sum
description: Swiggy's pricing tree: each node holds a price delta. A 'combo path' is a root-to-leaf path; combo total = sum of node values. Given level-order `tree` and `target`, return `true` if any root-to-leaf path sums to `target`.
hints: DFS subtracting node.val; at a leaf check remaining == 0.
Empty tree -> false (no path).
Leaf = no left and no right.

title: Razorpay All Root-to-Leaf Paths
description: Razorpay's audit needs every root-to-leaf path of merchant IDs. Given level-order `tree`, return all root-to-leaf paths (each as an array of values) in DFS preorder discovery order.
hints: DFS carrying the current path array.
Leaf: push a copy of path into output.
Backtrack: pop after recursing.

title: Flipkart BST Kth Smallest Price
description: Flipkart's BST holds product prices. Given the BST `tree` and integer `k`, return the kth smallest price (1-indexed).
hints: Inorder traversal of a BST gives values sorted ascending.
Stop early at the kth visit — saves work.
Iterative inorder with stack is the cleanest early-exit form.

title: PhonePe Sorted Merchants to Balanced BST
description: PhonePe has a sorted (ascending) array of merchant IDs. Build a height-balanced BST and return its level-order encoded array. Pick the *middle* element of the slice as root; with even-length slice pick the left middle (`floor((lo+hi)/2)`).
hints: Recursion: pick middle, build left from [lo, mid-1], right from [mid+1, hi].
Use floor((lo+hi)/2) for the middle.
Then serialize the resulting tree level-order.

title: Razorpay Insert Into BST
description: Insert a new merchant ID `val` into Razorpay's BST `tree`. Return the level-order array of the resulting tree. Insert as a leaf along the standard BST path. The value is guaranteed not to exist already.
hints: Recursion: if root null create node; else descend left or right.
Iterative pointer-walk also works.
Use treeToArray to serialize.

title: Zomato Flatten Menu Tree to List
description: Zomato wants a single-column rendering: flatten the binary tree in-place into a right-skewed list (each node's left = null, right = next in preorder). Given level-order `tree`, return the level-order array of the flattened tree.
hints: Reverse postorder trick: build right -> left -> root and stitch with prev pointer.
Or do a preorder collection then re-link.
After flattening every node has left=null, right=next.

title: Swiggy Right Side Restaurant View
description: Swiggy's mall view: imagine standing on the right side, you see the rightmost node at every level. Given level-order `tree`, return that list top-to-bottom.
hints: BFS: at each level, last dequeued node is the right view.
DFS preorder, right before left, recording first hit per depth, also works.
Empty -> [].

title: Razorpay Average Per Settlement Level
description: For each level of Razorpay's settlement tree, compute the average node value. Given level-order `tree`, return list of averages from root level downward.
hints: BFS by level — sum/count per level.
Use level size at the start of each iteration.
Floating-point division in JS is fine.

title: Flipkart Build Tree from Preorder + Inorder
description: Reconstruct Flipkart's category tree from its preorder and inorder traversals (all values unique). Return the level-order array of the resulting tree.
constraints: Both arrays describe the same tree
hints: First of preorder is root.
Find root in inorder — left of it is left subtree, right is right.
Use a Map<val, inorder index> for O(1) lookup.

title: Razorpay Max Payment Path Sum
description: Razorpay represents a fee-flow as a binary tree (signed integers). A path is any sequence of connected nodes (no node twice). Return the maximum sum over any path. Path can start and end anywhere; must contain at least one node.
hints: DFS returning best 'gain' from below (single chain).
At each node, candidate = node.val + max(0, leftGain) + max(0, rightGain).
Track global max separately from returned chain.

title: PhonePe Delete Merchant From BST
description: Delete a merchant ID `key` from PhonePe's BST `tree`. If the node has two children, replace its value with the **inorder successor** (smallest value in the right subtree) and delete that successor. Return the level-order array of the resulting BST. If `key` is absent, the tree is unchanged.
hints: Locate via BST descent.
0 or 1 child: replace with the surviving child (or null).
2 children: copy successor's value into node, then delete successor in right subtree.

title: Zomato Good Restaurants in Tree
description: Zomato calls a node 'good' if no node on the root-to-it path has a strictly greater value. Given level-order `tree`, return the count of good nodes.
hints: DFS carrying the maximum-so-far on the path.
If node.val >= maxSoFar, it's good; update max and recurse.
Root is always good.

title: Razorpay House Robber Tree
description: Razorpay's neighbourhood is a binary tree of houses with cash values. You can't rob two directly-connected houses (parent+child). Return the maximum cash you can rob.
hints: DFS returns pair [robThis, skipThis].
robThis = node.val + skipLeft + skipRight.
skipThis = max(robLeft, skipLeft) + max(robRight, skipRight).

title: Razorpay Serialize and Deserialize Tree
description: Razorpay needs to ship trees over the wire. Implement serialize+deserialize: input is a level-order array (with `null` for missing). Build the tree, then re-serialize it. Return the level-order array (same canonical form).
hints: arrayToTree handles deserialization (already provided).
treeToArray handles serialization.
Round-tripping should yield the same canonical level-order array.

title: Flipkart Category Tree Boundary
description:
Flipkart's UI shell wants the *boundary* of a binary tree in anti-clockwise order: (1) root, (2) left boundary nodes top-to-bottom (excluding leaves), (3) all leaves left-to-right, (4) right boundary nodes bottom-to-top (excluding leaves). If root is a leaf, return just `[root.val]`. Avoid duplicating the root.

Left boundary: starting from root.left, repeatedly go left if exists, else right; include each non-leaf node visited.
Right boundary: starting from root.right, repeatedly go right if exists, else left; include each non-leaf node visited (later reversed).
hints: Three pieces: left boundary (no leaves), all leaves L-to-R, reversed right boundary (no leaves).
Always start with root; single-node tree returns [root.val].
Be careful: do not double-count root as boundary AND leaf.

title: Swiggy Vertical Order Traversal
description: Swiggy's UI needs a vertical scan: group binary tree nodes by their column index (root = 0, left child = col-1, right = col+1). Output one array per column from leftmost to rightmost. Within a column, sort by depth ascending; ties (same col + depth) sorted by value ascending.
hints: BFS or DFS recording (col, depth, val).
Group by col, sort each group by (depth, val).
Or use a Map<col, list> with BFS so depth order falls out, then sort same-(col,depth) by val.

title: Razorpay Recover Swapped BST
description: A migration script accidentally swapped two values in Razorpay's BST. Restore the BST by swapping them back **in place**. Return the level-order array of the corrected tree.
hints: Inorder of a BST is strictly increasing.
Find the two violations during inorder; swap their values.
The two swapped nodes might be adjacent (one violation) or not (two violations).

title: PhonePe Complete Tree Node Count
description: PhonePe stores merchant priority in a *complete* binary tree (every level full except possibly the last, which is filled left-to-right). Given level-order `tree`, return the node count using the structure for an O((log n)^2) algorithm.
hints: Compute leftmost depth (lh) and rightmost depth (rh).
If lh == rh, subtree is full -> 2^lh - 1.
Otherwise recurse left + recurse right + 1.

title: Zomato Maximum Width of Tree
description: Zomato's render planner needs the maximum *width* of the binary tree. The width of a level is the number of positions between the leftmost and rightmost non-null nodes (counting null gaps in between as if it were a complete tree). Return the largest such width across all levels.
hints: BFS pairing each node with a positional index — left child = 2*i, right = 2*i+1.
At each level, width = lastIdx - firstIdx + 1.
Reset indices per level (subtract first idx) to avoid overflow.

title: Razorpay Longest Zigzag in Tree
description: A 'zigzag' path in Razorpay's binary tree alternates direction between left and right at each step. Length is counted in *edges*. Return the longest zigzag path length.
hints: DFS returning two values per node: longest zigzag path starting here going LEFT first, going RIGHT first.
If I start by going left, my next step is determined by my left child's right-zig.
Track global max separately.

title: Flipkart Preorder String Serialize/Deserialize
description: Flipkart compresses category trees as a preorder string with '#' marking null. Format: comma-separated, e.g. `1,2,#,#,3,#,#`. Implement a round-trip: take a level-order array `data`, build the tree, serialize to preorder string, deserialize that string, then return the resulting tree as a level-order array.
hints: Serialize: preorder DFS, append node.val (or '#') and a comma.
Deserialize: split on commas, consume tokens via an index pointer.
Round-trip should reproduce the canonical level-order array.

title: Razorpay Maximum Leaf-to-Leaf Path Sum
description: Razorpay needs the maximum sum among all leaf-to-leaf paths in a binary tree. The path must start and end at distinct leaves and may pass through any node. If the tree has fewer than 2 leaves, return -10000 as a sentinel.
hints: DFS returning the best root-to-leaf chain sum from a node downward.
At a node with TWO non-null children, candidate = leftBest + rightBest + node.val.
If only one child exists, propagate but don't update the global best yet.
